name: A Marronda - Alto Eo Route
name_original: Parque Natural A Marronda – circular desde A Cortevella
description: Hike the moderate 16.2-mile Parque Natural A Marronda circular from A Cortevella, exploring ancient forests, waterfalls, and river views.
region_intro: Hiking around Baleira offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and significant elevation changes. The region features a network of trails that traverse varied terrain, from long-distance paths with considerable climbs to more moderate routes. Hikers can expect to encounter a mix of natural beauty and historical points of interest throughout the area. Best hiking trails around Baleira The most popular hiking route is A Marronda - Alto Eo Route, a 16.2 miles (26.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete, leading through the A Marronda forest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Montouto Hospital and Dolmen – Paradavella Inn loop from Alto de Cerredo, a difficult 17.8 miles (28.7 km) path. This route features challenging ascents and passes by historical landmarks. Local hikers also love the Paradavella Inn – Montouto Hospital and Dolmen loop from Eiros, a 17.2 miles (27.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 8 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Baleira is defined by dense woodlands, rolling hills, and historic sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest paths to more difficult long-distance trails. The routes in Baleira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Baleira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Baleira?
A: Baleira offers a network of 6 hiking trails, ranging from moderate forest paths to more challenging long-distance routes. This includes 1 easy, 3 moderate, and 2 difficult trails.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Baleira?
A: Yes, Baleira features at least one easy hiking trail. While the region is known for its varied terrain and significant elevation changes, there are options suitable for those seeking a less strenuous experience or for families. For a moderate option that is not overly long, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrims' Refreshment Stand – Straw Hórreo loop from Pena do Trabeiro&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 12.7 miles (20.4 km) long.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes in Baleira?
A: Hike durations in Baleira vary significantly depending on the trail's length and difficulty. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40367620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Tomé loop from Castiñeiro do Neto&lt;/a&gt; can take around 4 hours 26 minutes, while longer, more difficult trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556790" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paradavella Inn – Montouto Hospital and Dolmen loop from Eiros&lt;/a&gt; can take over 8 hours.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the trails in Baleira?
A: Baleira's trails often lead through dense woodlands and rolling hills. You can also discover notable natural monuments such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5416282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Miño River&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906357" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seimeira de Vilagocende Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region's landscape is characterized by its natural beauty.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, Baleira is rich in historical sites. Many trails pass by significant landmarks, including the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4527080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montouto Hospital and Dolmen&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find several historic churches in the area, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4439474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Mary of Vilabade&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4439021" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago de Fontaneira&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Baleira?
A: The best time for hiking in Baleira generally spans from spring through autumn (April to October). During these months, the weather is typically milder and more stable, making for pleasant conditions to explore the diverse landscapes. Summers can be warm, while spring and autumn offer vibrant foliage and comfortable temperatures.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Baleira?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available, allowing you to start and end your hike at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39556816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montouto Hospital and Dolmen – Paradavella Inn loop from Alto de Cerredo&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40367620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint Tomé loop from Castiñeiro do Neto&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Baleira?
A: The hiking trails in Baleira are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's diverse landscape, which includes dense woodlands and rolling hills, as well as the historical points of interest encountered along the routes.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Baleira?
A: Parking availability can vary depending on the specific trailhead. For routes that start near villages or popular attractions, you may find designated parking areas. It is advisable to check local signage or route descriptions for specific parking information before you set out.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Baleira?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in rural areas like Baleira, especially if kept on a leash. However, regulations can vary, particularly in protected natural areas or near livestock. It's always best to observe local signage and be respectful of wildlife and other hikers.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking options in Baleira?
A: While Baleira offers a variety of trails, some routes, especially the longer and more challenging ones, tend to be less frequented. For a potentially quieter experience, consider exploring trails that venture deeper into the woodlands or those that are not highlighted as the most popular. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38170534" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrims' Refreshment Stand – Straw Hórreo loop from Pena do Trabeiro&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate challenge away from the busiest paths.
tips: Nice viewpoint surrounded by the Eo river that is very close to the A Cortevella recreational area, in the A Marronda Natural Park.
Beautiful waterfall near San Paio, in the A Marronda Natural Park. Without a doubt one of the most beautiful and impressive in the province of Lugo.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Parque Natural A Marronda circular from A Cortevella trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as moderate. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness due to varied terrain, including continuous ascents and descents. While some paths are accessible, certain sections can be challenging, especially the climb from A Cortevella to Mendreiras.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: The full circular hike is approximately 26 kilometers long and generally takes around 4 hours and 34 minutes to complete at a steady hiking pace. Keep in mind that this duration doesn't include stops for photos or rest.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a diverse mix of terrain, including tracks, traditional paths, and segments running alongside the Eo River. The route is predominantly unpaved, with some natural and a few paved sections. You may also cross streams and experience transitions from high ground with expansive views to dense forest trails.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail showcases the stunning Fraga da Marronda, an exceptional native Galician forest. You'll also pass by impressive waterfalls like the Pozo da Ferreira Waterfall and the Fervenza da Acea de Serra. The route follows the scenic Eo River, and you'll find the Muíño do Ferreiro Viewpoint offering great views. The A Cortevella Recreational Area also provides a glimpse into traditional rural architecture.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in Parque Natural A Marronda?
faqs.answer: The natural park is home to a variety of wildlife. Hikers might spot species such as wild boar, deer, foxes, and squirrels. The diverse forest environment also supports a rich variety of birds.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Parque Natural A Marronda circular from A Cortevella trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in A Cortevella. Parking is usually available near the A Cortevella Recreational Area, which serves as a key access point for the route.

faqs.question: How do I get to A Cortevella by public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to A Cortevella, especially for direct access to the trailhead, can be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes serving Baleira or nearby towns and plan for potential transfers or a taxi for the final leg to A Cortevella. Checking current timetables before your trip is recommended.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is beautiful year-round, but it is particularly stunning in autumn when the deciduous trees of the Fraga da Marronda display a vibrant palette of reds, oranges, and browns. Spring also offers lush greenery and blooming flora. Always check weather conditions before heading out.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails within natural parks in Galicia, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety and comfort of other hikers. Always practice responsible pet ownership by cleaning up after your dog.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in Parque Natural A Marronda?
faqs.answer: No, typically you do not need a specific permit or to pay an entrance fee to hike the trails within Parque Natural A Marronda, including this circular route from A Cortevella. However, as it is a Special Area of Conservation (ZEC), visitors are expected to respect all environmental regulations and guidelines to preserve its natural integrity.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While the full circular route is classified as moderate and can be challenging for beginners or young children due to its length and elevation changes, shorter, easier alternatives are available within the Parque Natural A Marronda. Families with younger children or those seeking a less strenuous walk might consider exploring only sections of the trail or looking for designated easy routes within the park.
